meta_title: Roofing in Century Village, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Century Village, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems, full replacements & repairs. Licensed, A+ BBB. Free estimates—call today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Solutions for Century Village Homeowners Who Deserve the Best
intro:
Century Village in West Palm Beach is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved active adult communities—a place where neighbors know each other by name, the Century Village Clubhouse hums with activity, and homeowners have spent decades building comfortable, well-earned lives. But beneath the vibrant community spirit lies a quiet challenge that nearly every resident here faces: aging roofs that were never built to handle the kind of storm intensity South Florida now regularly delivers. With approximately 80% of homes in Century Village constructed before 1994, the vast majority of rooftops predate the sweeping reforms brought about by Florida's post-Hurricane Andrew building code overhaul. That means most roofs in ZIP code 33409 were installed under older standards that simply don't meet today's requirements for wind resistance, water infiltration, or long-term durability in a hurricane-prone climate.

Located about eight miles inland from the coast, Century Village isn't directly on the water—but don't let that distance create a false sense of security. Palm Beach County sits squarely in a 140 mph wind zone, and the community has weathered no fewer than seven named storms since 1990, including the catastrophic back-to-back seasons of Hurricane Frances in 2004 and Hurricane Wilma in 2005, followed by Hurricane Irma in 2017. Each of those storms exposed the vulnerability of older roofing systems throughout the Century Village West Palm Beach area, leaving homeowners scrambling for repairs and contractors. The reality is that inland communities can experience hurricane-force gusts, destructive wind-driven rain, and significant uplift damage just as seriously as coastal neighborhoods during a major storm event.

For homeowners in Century Village, where the average home value sits around $160,000, a failing or outdated roof isn't just a safety hazard—it's a direct threat to one of your most important financial assets. A damaged or underperforming roof can accelerate interior moisture damage, spike energy bills, trigger insurance non-renewals, and dramatically reduce resale value. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, proudly known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' specializes in helping Century Village homeowners replace aging rooftops with modern, hurricane-rated systems that protect their homes, lower their insurance premiums, and stand up to whatever the Atlantic hurricane season delivers. Whether you need a full roof replacement or targeted storm damage repairs, our team is ready to serve this community with the expertise, quality products, and genuine care it deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Century Village Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation one roof at a time across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand that Century Village homeowners have unique needs. This is a community of discerning residents who have seen contractors come and go, who ask smart questions, and who expect professionalism from the first phone call to the final inspection. That's exactly why our approach is different: we handle every single aspect of your roofing project in-house, with our own trained and certified crews—no subcontractors, no strangers on your roof, no finger-pointing when questions arise. Our team brings more than 60 years of combined roofing experience to every project in the 33409 area, and that institutional knowledge shows in every detail of how we work.

With over 500 homes completed throughout South Florida, we've encountered virtually every roofing challenge that older construction can present—from deteriorating underlayment on mid-1970s flat-tile roofs to wind-lifted shingles on homes that were re-roofed before the current Florida Building Code was fully implemented. When we assess a Century Village roof, we're not just looking at the top layer. We inspect the decking, the flashing, the fascia, the underlayment, and the ventilation system, because a truly durable roof is a complete system, not just a layer of shingles or tile. Our thorough inspection process means we catch problems that other contractors miss—and we fix them correctly the first time.

Being A+ rated by the Better Business Bureau and fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida isn't just a credential for us—it's a daily commitment to the homeowners who trust us. We pull every required permit through the Palm Beach County Building Division, we schedule and pass all required inspections, and we never cut corners on materials or installation techniques. For seniors and fixed-income residents in Century Village who can't afford the disruption of a failed roof job, our reliability and accountability aren't just nice to have—they're essential. We also offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it genuinely possible to protect your home without draining your savings.

section_history.h2: A Community Tested by Storms: Century Village's Hurricane History and What It Means for Your Roof
section_history.body:
Century Village has not been spared by South Florida's turbulent storm history. In 2004, Hurricane Frances swept through Palm Beach County as a Category 2 storm, bringing sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that punished older rooftops throughout West Palm Beach. Many homes in the Century Village area experienced missing shingles, damaged underlayment, and in some cases, structural decking issues that took months to fully remediate. Less than a year later, Hurricane Wilma—one of the most powerful Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded—carved a path directly across South Florida in October 2005, delivering rapid and intense wind gusts that exceeded 100 mph across much of Palm Beach County. The damage in Century Village was significant, and the resulting demand for roofing contractors created backlogs that left some homeowners with temporary repairs for months.

More than a decade later, Hurricane Irma in September 2017 served as another sobering reminder that no community in South Florida is truly out of harm's way. Though Irma's strongest impacts were felt along the west coast of Florida, its outer bands produced damaging winds across Palm Beach County, and homes with roofs that had been patched but never properly replaced showed their vulnerabilities. The cumulative toll of Frances, Wilma, Irma, and the four other named storms that have affected this area since 1990 tells a clear story: older roofing systems in Century Village have been stressed repeatedly, and many are well past their reliable service life. For homeowners who have been putting off a full replacement, each storm season represents another roll of the dice. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps you stop gambling with your home's most important protective system and instead invest in a roof built to last through whatever comes next.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Century Village and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Century Village falls outside Florida's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which covers Miami-Dade and Broward counties, but that distinction does not mean roofing standards here are relaxed. Palm Beach County enforces the Florida Building Code in full, and for a community located in a 140 mph wind zone, the requirements for roofing products, installation methods, and fastening schedules are strict and non-negotiable. All roofing materials installed in Century Village must carry appropriate Miami-Dade or Florida Product Approval ratings, demonstrate resistance to the wind speeds applicable to this zone, and be installed according to the manufacturer's specifications and code-compliant nailing patterns. Using non-approved products or improper fastening techniques not only creates safety risks—it can also void your material warranty and lead to failed inspections.

The permit process for roof replacements in Century Village runs through the Palm Beach County Building Division, and it is not optional. A proper roofing permit ensures that your project is reviewed for code compliance and inspected at key stages of the installation process. Homeowners who allow contractors to work without permits face serious consequences: unpermitted work can complicate home sales, result in fines, and may even require tear-off and reinstallation at the homeowner's expense. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we manage the entire permitting process on your behalf from application through final inspection, so you never have to worry about compliance gaps.

For pre-1994 homes—which represent the overwhelming majority of Century Village's housing stock—a full roof replacement often triggers a requirement to bring the entire roofing system up to current code standards, including updated flashing, proper drip edge installation, and in some cases, decking repairs or replacement. Our crews are fully trained in current Florida Building Code requirements and work in close coordination with Palm Beach County inspectors to ensure every project meets or exceeds all applicable standards. When we're done, your roof is not just new—it's legal, documented, and code-compliant.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake Roofing Systems: Premium Products Built for Century Village Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted roofing brands in the industry, and both are ideally suited to the conditions Century Village homeowners face. For homes with asphalt shingle roofing—which is common throughout the Century Village West Palm Beach community—we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles, a product line renowned for its dimensional beauty, long-term durability, and exceptional wind resistance. The Landmark Series is engineered to meet Class A fire ratings and carries wind resistance ratings that align with Palm Beach County's 140 mph wind zone requirements. These shingles feature a dual-layer construction and are available in a wide range of color profiles that complement the architectural styles common in this community. CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty program provides comprehensive coverage that protects your investment for decades, and the company's reputation for consistent quality makes it a product we're proud to stand behind.

For Century Village homeowners with tile roofing systems—whether they're currently living with aging clay or concrete tile that's cracked, slipping, or beyond repair—we install West Lake roofing systems, featuring both concrete and clay tile options. West Lake tile is manufactured to meet Florida's demanding impact and wind uplift requirements, and their profiles are designed to replicate the classic Mediterranean aesthetics that many Century Village homes were originally built with. Tile roofing is particularly well-suited to South Florida's climate because of its superior thermal mass, natural resistance to the sun's UV rays, and exceptional longevity—a properly installed tile roof can last 40 to 50 years with routine maintenance.

Whether your Century Village home calls for CertainTeed shingles or a West Lake tile system, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house installation teams are factory-trained and certified to install these products to the manufacturer's exact specifications. Proper installation is the difference between a roof that performs as promised and one that fails prematurely, and our commitment to doing the job right every time is why we've earned the trust of hundreds of homeowners across Palm Beach County.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Century Village? Honest Pricing and Flexible Financing
section_cost.body:
One of the most common concerns we hear from Century Village homeowners is about cost—and it's a completely understandable concern, especially in a community where many residents are living on fixed incomes and working to protect home values that average around $160,000. The honest answer is that roofing costs vary based on the size of your home, the roofing material you choose, the condition of the existing decking and underlayment, and the complexity of the installation. In Century Village, most full roof replacements with CertainTeed architectural shingles typically range from $8,000 to $15,000, while tile roofing systems tend to run higher due to material costs and the additional structural requirements. Every estimate we provide is detailed, transparent, and based on a thorough assessment of your specific home—never a vague ballpark designed to get a signature.

For homeowners who are concerned about fitting a roof replacement into a tight budget, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options designed to make quality roofing accessible to everyone. Our programs start at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. That means you don't have to drain your savings or delay a necessary replacement while your home sustains ongoing weather damage. Spreading the cost of a new roof over manageable monthly payments makes it possible to protect your home now and pay for it comfortably over time.

Beyond the upfront cost, it's important to consider the financial return on a quality roof replacement. A new, code-compliant roof can immediately increase the market value and insurability of your Century Village home. Homes with updated roofing systems are more attractive to buyers, easier to insure, and often qualify for significantly reduced homeowner's insurance premiums. In many cases, the annual insurance savings alone can offset a meaningful portion of the financing cost, making a new roof one of the smartest investments a Century Village homeowner can make.

section_insurance.h2: Maximize Your Homeowner's Insurance Savings with a New Roof in Century Village
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Palm Beach County has become increasingly expensive and increasingly difficult to maintain as insurers reassess their risk exposure throughout South Florida. For Century Village residents—particularly those with older roofs—the insurance implications are significant. Many major carriers are declining to renew policies on homes with roofs more than 15 to 20 years old, or they're offering only limited coverage at dramatically higher premiums. If your roof is approaching or past that threshold, you may already have received a non-renewal notice or a request for a roof inspection. A full replacement with a modern, code-compliant roofing system is often the most direct path to restoring or maintaining comprehensive insurance coverage.

Beyond simply maintaining coverage, a new roof can unlock substantial insurance discounts through the Florida wind mitigation credit system. After your new roof is installed, you can commission a licensed wind mitigation inspector to document the features of your roof—including the shape, the fastening schedule, the type of underlayment, and whether secondary water resistance was applied. These features are submitted to your insurance carrier, and the credits can result in meaningful premium reductions. For Century Village homeowners, where every dollar counts, wind mitigation savings of 10% to 40% on the wind portion of a homeowner's insurance premium are not uncommon following a quality roof replacement.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works with homeowners throughout the insurance process. We document our installations thoroughly so that wind mitigation inspectors have all the information they need, and we can walk you through what to expect from the inspection process. Common carriers operating in Palm Beach County—including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage—all recognize wind mitigation credits, and our team's familiarity with what inspectors look for means our installations are completed in a way that maximizes your potential savings. Investing in a new roof with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing isn't just about protection—it's about financial peace of mind for the years ahead.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Century Village home's roof needs to be replaced versus repaired?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 20 years old—which applies to the majority of homes in Century Village given the community's pre-1994 construction—a full replacement is typically more cost-effective than ongoing repairs. Signs that replacement is necessary include widespread granule loss on shingles, cracked or slipping tile, sagging decking, persistent interior leaks after storms, or a roof that has already been repaired multiple times. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections and will give you an honest assessment of whether repair or replacement makes more sense for your specific situation.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Century Village, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Roof replacement costs in Century Village typically range from $8,000 to $15,000 for asphalt shingle systems and can be higher for tile roofing, depending on the size and complexity of the job. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners, making it possible to protect your home without depleting your savings. Every estimate is free, detailed, and tailored to your specific roof—no surprise charges or hidden fees.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Century Village, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements in Century Village, and the permit is issued through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Unpermitted roofing work can result in fines, complications during home sales, and potential requirements to redo the work at your expense. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf—from application submission to scheduling inspections and obtaining final approval—so you can have complete confidence that your project is fully compliant with Palm Beach County and Florida Building Code requirements.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical Century Village home?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on Century Village homes are completed within one to three days, depending on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, and whether any decking repairs are needed. Tile roofing projects may take slightly longer due to the additional complexity of tile installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing schedules projects efficiently and works with dedicated in-house crews—not subcontractors—so there are no handoff delays. We'll give you a clear timeline before work begins and keep you informed throughout the process.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Century Village?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes—significantly. A new, code-compliant roof qualifies your home for a wind mitigation inspection, and the resulting report can unlock premium discounts of 10% to 40% on the wind portion of your policy through Florida's wind mitigation credit system. Additionally, many insurers in Palm Beach County will not renew or will surcharge policies on homes with older roofs, so replacing an aging roof may restore your access to competitive insurance rates altogether. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs roofs in a way that maximizes wind mitigation credit eligibility, helping you get the most out of your insurance savings.

cta_headline: Century Village Homeowners: Get a Free Roof Inspection from The Kings of Service
cta_body: Don't wait for the next storm season to find out what your roof can't handle—contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof inspection and estimate, and let us show Century Village why we've earned our reputation as South Florida's most trusted roofing team.
